articles.title: YOU DON'T NEED A WHOLE NEW DATABASE FOR AI
articles.summary: You used to be able to run your entire app on a SQL database. But with RAG and AI, you need vector search capabilities—and managing a separate vector database is a pain.

articles.title: THE MULTI-REPOSITORY TYPESCRIPT PROBLEM
articles.summary: While monorepos can easily share TypeScript types across services, polyrepos face complex issues with type synchronization, version management, and the "recursive type discovery problem," where extracting types reveals cascading dependencies and naming conflicts. Tools like ts-morph programmatically extract and compare types across repository boundaries.

articles.title: MAKING SENSE OF THE PERFORMANCE EXTENSIBILITY API
DEVELOPERS, REINVENTED
articles.summary: GitHub's CEO writes about how the developer role is being reinvented due to AI. These developers are moving through stages, from initial skepticism to becoming AI collaborators and strategists, focusing on delegation and verification rather than direct coding. AI will probably write a significant portion of code in the near future, leading to a transformation where their value lies in orchestrating AI agents and innovating beyond implementation.

articles.title: NO, AI IS NOT MAKING ENGINEERS 10X AS PRODUCTIVE
AGENTIC CODING THINGS THAT DIDN'T WORK
articles.summary: Most of this dev's attempts at complex agentic workflows didn't stick, leading him to delete failed automations and simplify his approach. His most effective workflow ended up being 95% just talking to the AI through speech-to-text and providing more context, rather than using elaborate slash commands, hooks, or print mode features.

articles.title: OPEN MODELS BY OPENAI
articles.summary: OpenAI has released two new open-weight reasoning models, gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b, designed for various use cases and deployment environments. These models are licensed under Apache 2.0, making them customizable and suitable for commercial use, with features like agentic task capabilities and full chain-of-thought access.

articles.title: HARMONY
articles.summary: OpenAI uses the Harmony response format in its `gpt-oss` open-weight model series, which enables structured conversation outputs. The company has provided a Rust-based core with Python bindings for rendering and parsing the Harmony format.

articles.title: CLAUDE OPUS 4.1
articles.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.1, an upgraded version of Claude Opus 4 with improvements in agentic tasks, real-world coding, and reasoning capabilities. This new model achieves a coding performance of 74.5% on SWE-bench Verified and is great at precise code correction and debugging.

articles.title: INJECTING JAVA FROM NATIVE LIBRARIES ON ANDROID
articles.summary: On Android, accessing advanced phone capabilities like Bluetooth or certain network features from Rust requires using the Java SDK via JNI because the NDK and Libc lack direct APIs. While JNI allows calling Java from Rust, traditionally, creating new Java classes from Rust was thought to be impossible. However, using `DexClassLoader`, Rust libraries can compile and inject necessary Java classes at runtime, making them more modular and easier for other developers to use.

articles.title: HOW FAR CAN WE PUSH AI AUTONOMY IN CODE GENERATION?
articles.summary: Thoughtworks experimented with generative AI to autonomously build a Spring Boot application using strategies like reusable prompts and reference applications. While the AI could generate simple applications, issues came up with increasing complexity, including adding unintended features, making incorrect assumptions, and declaring success despite failing tests. Human oversight was essential for high-quality, maintainable code.

articles.title: 2025 STACK OVERFLOW DEVELOPER SURVEY
articles.summary: The Stack Overflow 2025 Developer Survey collected data from over 49,000 developers across 177 countries, focusing on technology usage, AI trends, and developer satisfaction. There is increasing AI tool adoption, with 84% of developers using or planning to use them, but also declining positive sentiment towards AI and distrust in its accuracy. Python's adoption has significantly grown, while Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio remain the top IDEs.

articles.title: GENIE 3: A NEW FRONTIER FOR WORLD MODELS
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's Genie 3 is a new, real-time world model that generates diverse and interactive environments from text prompts, allowing users to navigate and interact within consistent, simulated worlds.

articles.title: WHY IS GITHUB UI GETTING SO MUCH SLOWER?
articles.summary: The increasingly slow performance of GitHub's user interface is due to inefficient client-side routing and excessive DOM node rendering.

articles.title: YOU CAN SPOT BASE64 ENCODED JSON, CERTIFICATES, AND PRIVATE KEYS
articles.summary: A trick to visually identify base64 encoded JSON is that they start with "ey", while certificates and private keys start with "LS" within files.

articles.title: TAMBO
articles.summary: Tambo is a React package that lets devs build AI-powered apps with generative UIs by registering React components and tools for use by LLMs.
